Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualisation

RunSCssGSEA.Rd

Runs per-cell single-sample Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (ssGSEA) on a Seurat object using GSVA (compatible with both GSVA v1 and v2 APIs), computes group-level significance (ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis), saves enrichment scores and significance tables as CSVs, and produces a ComplexHeatmap PDF of mean z-scored enrichment per group.

Usage

RunSCssGSEA(
  seurat_object,
  group.by,
  split.by = NULL,
  subset_cells = FALSE,
  subset_by = NULL,
  subset_values = NULL,
  gene_sets = NULL,
  deg_df = NULL,
  deg_gene_column = "feature",
  deg_group_column = "group",
  deg_fc_column = "logFC",
  deg_padj_column = "padj",
  deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,
  deg_top_n = 20,
  gene_set_library = NULL,
  gene_set_subcategory = NULL,
  search_terms = NULL,
  species = "Homo sapiens",
  method = "gsva",
  downsample = NULL,
  min.size = 5,
  ssgsea.norm = TRUE,
  ucell_max_rank = 1500L,
  cores = 1,
  chunk_size = 1000L,
  resume = FALSE,
  fit = "ANOVA",
  sig_group_by = NULL,
  p_cutoff = 0.05,
  show_only_significant = TRUE,
  group_colors = NULL,
  split_colors = NULL,
  heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,
    row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),
  add_boxplots = TRUE,
  add_pvalues = TRUE,
  add_to_object = FALSE,
  output_dir,
  object_name = "Analysis",
  subset_name = ""
)

Arguments

seurat_object

A Seurat object.

group.by

Character. Metadata column used as the primary grouping variable for the heatmap columns and significance testing (e.g. "Assignment", "celltype").

split.by

Character or NULL. Optional secondary metadata column for splitting heatmap columns (e.g. "Health", "Condition"). When set, significance is also tested within each split level in addition to the pooled test.

gene_sets

Named list of gene vectors (Mode 1). NULL if using another mode.

deg_df

Data frame of DEG results (Mode 2). NULL otherwise. Compatible with standard outputs from presto::wilcoxauc(), edgeR, or any table with gene, group, and log-fold-change columns.

deg_gene_column

Character. Column in deg_df holding gene symbols. Default "feature".

deg_group_column

Character scalar or length-2 character vector. Column(s) in deg_df defining the gene-set groups. One column → one gene set per unique value. Two columns → gene sets named "col1__NKSEP__col2"; heatmap rows split by col1 automatically. Default "group".

deg_fc_column

Character. Log-fold-change column for selecting top genes. Default "logFC".

deg_padj_column

Character or NULL. Adjusted p-value column for pre- filtering. NULL skips filtering. Default "padj".

deg_padj_cutoff

Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold for DEG pre-filtering. Default 0.05.

deg_top_n

Integer. Top N genes per group (by deg_fc_column) to include in each gene set. Default 20.

gene_set_library

Character or NULL. MSigDB collection code (Mode 3 or 4). Common options:

  • "H" — Hallmark (50 coherent biological processes)

  • "C2" — Curated: canonical pathways (combine with gene_set_subcategory = "CP:KEGG", "CP:REACTOME", "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS", "CP:BIOCARTA", "CP:PID")

  • "C5" — Gene Ontology (subcategory = "GO:BP", "GO:MF", "GO:CC")

  • "C6" — Oncogenic signatures

  • "C7" — Immunologic signatures (IMMUNESIGDB)

  • "C8" — Cell-type signatures

NULL fetches all collections (slow; use with search_terms).

gene_set_subcategory

Character or NULL. MSigDB sub-collection code. Default NULL (entire collection).

search_terms

Search filter applied to MSigDB gene-set names (case- insensitive, regex). Accepts two forms:

Character vector — OR search. Any gene set whose name matches at least one term is kept: search_terms = c("APOPTOSIS", "CELL_DEATH").

List of character vectors — AND/OR search. Each list element is an AND-group: all terms in that element must appear in the same pathway name (order and separation do not matter). Results are the union (OR) across AND-groups. Examples:

  • list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION")) — both words in same name

  • list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION"), "APOPTOSIS") — previous OR APOPTOSIS

  • list(c("T_CELL","ACTIVATION"), c("B_CELL","DIFFERENTIATION")) — two independent AND-groups, union of results

NULL (default) keeps all gene sets in the fetched collection.

species

Character. Species for MSigDB fetching. Passed to msigdbr::msigdbr(). Default "Homo sapiens". Use "Mus musculus" for mouse datasets. See msigdbr::msigdbr_species() for all supported species.

downsample

Integer or NULL. Maximum cells per group.by identity to use for scoring. ssGSEA is \(O(n_{\text{cells}})\) — downsampling to 500–2000 cells per group dramatically reduces runtime with minimal loss of group-level signal. NULL uses all cells. Default NULL.

min.size

Integer. Minimum gene-set size after intersecting with the expression matrix. Gene sets below this threshold are silently dropped. Default 5.

ssgsea.norm

Logical. Normalise ssGSEA enrichment scores to the [0, 1] range? Default TRUE (matches escape::enrichIt default).

cores

Integer. Number of parallel cores for GSVA. Requires BiocParallel. Falls back to serial if unavailable. Default 1.

fit

Character. Significance test applied per pathway: "ANOVA" (default, parametric F-test) or "Kruskal" (non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis). Both use BH adjustment for multiple testing.

sig_group_by

Character or NULL. Metadata column to use as the grouping factor for significance testing. NULL (default) uses group.by. Override when your display grouping (group.by) differs from your statistical grouping (e.g. display by fine cell type, test by broad disease category).

p_cutoff

Numeric. BH-adjusted p-value threshold for selecting pathways to display in the heatmap. Default 0.05.

show_only_significant

Logical. If TRUE, heatmap shows only pathways with p_adj < p_cutoff. Falls back to top 30 by statistic if no pathways pass the threshold. If FALSE, all pathways are shown. Default TRUE.

add_to_object

Logical. If FALSE (default), returns the enrichment scores data frame invisibly and does not modify the Seurat object (scores are saved to CSV only — recommended for large datasets to avoid metadata bloat). If TRUE, adds one metadata column per gene set to seurat_object@meta.data and returns the modified Seurat object. When downsample is also set, cells not included in the downsampled scoring set receive NA.

output_dir

Character. Directory for output files (created if absent).

object_name

Character. First component of output file name prefix. Default "Analysis".

subset_name

Character. Second component of output file name prefix. Default "".

Value

If add_to_object = FALSE (default): invisibly returns a data frame of enrichment scores (cells × gene sets) with additional columns for group.by and split.by identity.

If add_to_object = TRUE: invisibly returns the Seurat object with one metadata column per gene set.

Files saved to output_dir:

  • <prefix> ssGSEA_scores.csv — per-cell enrichment scores + group labels

  • <prefix> ssGSEA_significance.csv — per-pathway significance (pooled across all cells)

  • <prefix> ssGSEA_significance_by_<split.by>.csv — per-pathway significance within each split.by level (only when split.by is set)

  • <prefix> ssGSEA heatmap.pdf — ComplexHeatmap of mean z-scored enrichment per group

Details

Unlike pseudobulk ssGSEA (see RunGSEA_pseudobulk()), this function scores every cell individually rather than aggregating by sample first. It is appropriate when you have no meaningful biological replicates (sample IDs), or when you want to use the single-cell resolution for visualisation. For datasets with proper replicates, pseudobulk ssGSEA is statistically more rigorous.

Gene-set input (choose exactly one mode)

Mode 1 — pre-built named list

gene_sets = list(SetA = c("GENE1", "GENE2"), SetB = c("GENE3"))

Mode 2 — DEG data frame
Converts a marker/DEG table into one gene set per group. The deg_group_column argument can name one column (simple group, e.g. "celltype") or two columns (e.g. c("celltype", "cytokine")). When two columns are supplied, gene set names are built as "celltype__NKSEP__cytokine" internally and the heatmap rows are automatically split by the first column (celltype).

Mode 3 — MSigDB collection

gene_set_library = "H"                        # Hallmark
gene_set_library = "C2", gene_set_subcategory = "CP:KEGG"

Mode 4 — MSigDB term search (fetch all gene sets matching a pattern)

search_terms = c("CELL_DEATH", "APOPTOSIS")
# optionally restrict to a collection:
gene_set_library = "C2", search_terms = "CELL_DEATH"

On this page
